message:
feat(ffs-cli): major expansion of CLI command surface and functionality

Substantially expand the ffs-cli binary from a minimal skeleton into a
full-featured filesystem management tool (1112 insertions, 94 deletions):

- Implement comprehensive subcommand routing for mount, unmount, status,
  check, repair, snapshot, and diagnostic operations
- Add structured output formatting (JSON and human-readable table modes)
- Implement progress reporting for long-running operations with
  configurable verbosity levels
- Add filesystem health check and repair workflows with detailed
  reporting of block group state, extent tree integrity, and journal
  consistency
- Wire up snapshot create/list/delete/restore commands with proper
  MVCC version tracking

Also updates:
- FEATURE_PARITY.md: mark CLI expansion milestones as complete
- .beads/issues.jsonl: sync issue tracker with closed tasks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-13i9","title":"Implement FUSE statfs, fallocate, link, and symlink operations","description":"# Implement FUSE statfs, fallocate, link, and symlink operations

## GOAL
Implement the remaining FUSE operations needed for full filesystem functionality.

## OPERATIONS

### 1. statfs - Filesystem statistics
```rust
fn statfs(&self, ino: u64, reply: fuser::ReplyStatfs) {
    // Return filesystem statistics:
    // - blocks: total blocks
    // - bfree: free blocks
    // - bavail: available blocks (to non-root)
    // - files: total inodes
    // - ffree: free inodes
    // - bsize: block size
    // - namelen: max filename length
    // - frsize: fragment size
    
    let sb = self.fs.superblock();
    reply.statfs(
        sb.blocks_count,
        sb.free_blocks_count,
        sb.free_blocks_count, // available = free for now
        sb.inodes_count,
        sb.free_inodes_count,
        sb.block_size,
        255, // max name length
        sb.block_size,
    );
}
```

### 2. fallocate - Preallocate space
```rust
fn fallocate(
    &self, 
    ino: u64, 
    fh: u64, 
    offset: i64, 
    length: i64, 
    mode: i32, 
    reply: fuser::ReplyEmpty
) {
    // Modes:
    // - 0: allocate space
    // - FALLOC_FL_KEEP_SIZE: allocate but don't change size
    // - FALLOC_FL_PUNCH_HOLE: deallocate (with KEEP_SIZE)
    
    // 1. Validate mode
    // 2. Calculate block range
    // 3. Allocate/deallocate blocks via extent tree
    // 4. Update inode if needed
}
```

### 3. link - Create hard link
```rust
fn link(
    &self,
    ino: u64,
    newparent: u64,
    newname: &OsStr,
    reply: fuser::ReplyEntry,
) {
    // 1. Validate source inode exists
    // 2. Check link count limit (65000 for ext4)
    // 3. Add directory entry in newparent
    // 4. Increment link count on source inode
    // 5. Return entry attributes
}
```

### 4. symlink - Create symbolic link
```rust
fn symlink(
    &self,
    parent: u64,
    name: &OsStr,
    link: &Path,
    reply: fuser::ReplyEntry,
) {
    // 1. Allocate inode with S_IFLNK mode
    // 2. Store link target:
    //    - inline if len <= 60 bytes (ext4_inode.i_block)
    //    - in data block if longer
    // 3. Add directory entry
    // 4. Return entry attributes
}
```

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Unit: statfs returns correct values
2. Unit: fallocate allocates blocks without writing data
3. Unit: fallocate punch_hole removes blocks
4. Unit: link increments link count
5. Unit: link fails at limit (EMLINK)
6. Unit: symlink with short target stores inline
7. Unit: symlink with long target uses block
8. Integration: df shows correct space
9. Integration: hardlinks share data

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- statfs (trace): returned values
- fallocate (debug): inode, offset, length, mode, blocks allocated
- link (debug): source ino, parent, name, new link count
- symlink (debug): parent, name, target, storage mode

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. df command shows correct filesystem usage
2. fallocate preallocates without writing zeros
3. Hard links share underlying data
4. Symlinks resolve correctly
5. All ops return correct errno on error","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:16:03.821187089Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T19:33:15.741932171Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T19:33:15.741910711Z","close_reason":"Implemented statfs/link/symlink/fallocate paths; added EMLINK hard-link limit test; gates passed via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","ext4","fuse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-13i9","depends_on_id":"bd-2ad","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:52:34.187931677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-13i9","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:38.079636026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-13i9","depends_on_id":"bd-huh.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:16:10.642267335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":185,"issue_id":"bd-13i9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-14c","title":"Add performance regression test with baseline tracking","description":"# Add performance regression test with baseline tracking

## GOAL
Implement automated performance regression detection so no commit silently degrades performance.

## BACKGROUND
From AGENTS.md:
- Benchmark loop is mandatory
- Baseline first, then optimize
- Re-measure and record deltas

We need automated CI integration.

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Baseline Storage
```
benchmarks/
├── baselines/
│   ├── latest.json           # Current baseline
│   └── history/
│       ├── 2026-02-12.json
│       └── ...
├── results/
│   └── <commit-sha>.json
└── thresholds.toml           # Regression thresholds
```

### 2. Benchmark Suite
```rust
// In ffs-harness
criterion_group!(
    perf_regression,
    bench_read_throughput,
    bench_write_throughput,
    bench_lookup_latency,
    bench_readdir_latency,
    bench_mvcc_snapshot,
    bench_mvcc_commit,
    bench_arc_hit,
    bench_arc_miss,
);
```

### 3. Regression Detection
```toml
# thresholds.toml
[thresholds]
read_throughput = { min_ratio = 0.9 }  # max 10% regression
write_throughput = { min_ratio = 0.85 }
lookup_latency = { max_ratio = 1.1 }   # max 10% regression
```

### 4. CI Integration
```yaml
# .github/workflows/perf.yml
- name: Run benchmarks
  run: cargo bench -p ffs-harness -- --save-baseline current

- name: Check regression
  run: scripts/check_perf_regression.sh current baselines/latest.json
```

### 5. Reporting
- JSON output with all metrics
- Human-readable summary
- PR comment with delta table

## TESTS
1. Unit: Threshold checking logic
2. Integration: Benchmark suite runs to completion
3. Integration: Regression detected when introduced

## LOGGING
- Benchmark results (info): metric, value, baseline, ratio
- Regression (warn): metric, threshold exceeded
- Pass (info): all within threshold

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. CI fails on performance regression beyond threshold
2. Baselines updated on main branch
3. History preserved for trend analysis
4. Clear reporting of which metric regressed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleElk","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:54:00.578131962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T08:34:38.376815471Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T08:34:38.376796455Z","close_reason":"Completed: added TOML-driven perf thresholds, benchmark baseline latest/history exports, CI artifact wiring, and threshold classification unit tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","harness","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-14c","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:40.091863809Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-14c","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:29.732136146Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":192,"issue_id":"bd-14c","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-14jb","title":"EPIC: EBR for Block Version GC (Graveyard Entry 14.10)","description":"# EPIC: EBR for Block Version GC (Graveyard Entry 14.10)

## PURPOSE
Implement Epoch-Based Reclamation (EBR) for block version chain garbage collection. Without GC, MVCC version chains grow unbounded — every committed write creates a new version that is never freed. EBR provides safe, lock-free reclamation of old versions once no active transaction can observe them.

## BACKGROUND
From the Alien CS Graveyard entry 14.10:
- EBR: threads announce entry into epochs; old block versions freed after all threads advance past the epoch in which versions were retired
- Critical for MVCC scalability — without GC, memory usage is O(total_writes) instead of O(active_versions)
- crossbeam-epoch provides production-quality EBR in Rust

## DESIGN

### Epoch Lifecycle
1. Each MVCC reader pins the current epoch on transaction begin
2. Writers create new versions and retire old versions (mark for deferred free)
3. Background GC thread advances the global epoch periodically
4. Retired versions are freed when all threads have advanced past the retire epoch

### Version Chain Integration
- Version chain: Block -> V3 -> V2 -> V1 (newest first)
- Active snapshot with commit_seq=5 may still need V2
- V1 can be freed only when no active snapshot has commit_seq <= V1.commit_seq
- EBR epoch pins ensure no version is freed while observable

### crossbeam-epoch Integration
- Use crossbeam::epoch::{pin, Guard, Atomic, Owned}
- Version chain nodes wrapped in Atomic<VersionNode>
- On retire: guard.defer_destroy(old_version)
- GC happens automatically when guard is dropped and epoch advances

### Memory Bounds
- Version chain length bound: configurable max versions per block (e.g., 64)
- If chain exceeds bound: force-advance oldest snapshot or abort oldest transaction
- This prevents unbounded memory growth even with long-running snapshots

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Old block versions are freed after all observing transactions complete
- [ ] No use-after-free (verified with Miri or AddressSanitizer)
- [ ] Memory usage bounded: version chain length enforced
- [ ] crossbeam-epoch correctly integrated
- [ ] Benchmark: memory usage stable under continuous write load
- [ ] Unit tests: version freed after last observer drops
- [ ] Unit tests: version NOT freed while observer holds pin

## Success Criteria
1. All children closed
2. E2E GC test (bd-14jb.4) passes
3. No memory leaks under continuous write workload
4. Miri clean (no undefined behavior)
5. Version chain length bounded","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"BlueElm","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:22:59.588504156Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T19:06:08.289309228Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T19:06:08.289282488Z","close_reason":"All child beads closed with evidence for EBR integration, bounded reclamation, benchmark + E2E + unit/Miri validation, and cross-crate flush/GC coordination.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ebr","gc","mvcc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-14jb","depends_on_id":"bd-22w","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:39.665139266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":94,"issue_id":"bd-14jb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW (lint fix): Epic is missing ## Success Criteria section. EV score: 13.333333333333334 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: S/A
Budgeted mode: max_chain_len=64, wal_flush_batch=256, dirty_ratio_cap=0.8
Fallback trigger: single-writer conservative mode with bounded queue

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:

- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:20Z"},{"id":245,"issue_id":"bd-14jb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closure verification: all child beads are closed and collectively satisfy epic criteria. Evidence map: (1) crossbeam-epoch integration + retire/reclaim wiring: bd-14jb.1; (2) chain-length bounds and backpressure: bd-14jb.2; (3) memory benchmark scenarios with EBR metrics artifact: bd-14jb.3; (4) E2E concurrent GC correctness and pin/unpin safety with Miri coverage: bd-14jb.4; (5) cross-crate write-back flush guard + GC integration proof test: bd-14jb.5; (6) unit tests for reclamation and snapshot-pin gating with Miri coverage: bd-14jb.6; (7) CLI observability for block/EBR stats: bd-14jb.7. Epic success criteria check: all children closed ✅, E2E GC test bead closed ✅, bounded chain/memory evidence from .2/.3 ✅, Miri evidence captured in child close reasons ✅.","created_at":"2026-02-18T19:06:04Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2r8","title":"Implement adaptive backpressure system with budget propagation","description":"# Implement adaptive backpressure system with budget propagation

## GOAL
Implement Cx-based budget system that propagates backpressure through the stack, preventing cascade failures under load.

## BACKGROUND (Alien-Artifact Quality)
Traditional systems fail abruptly under load. FrankenFS must degrade gracefully using information-theoretic principles:

1. **Budget = scarce resource allocation**
   - deadline: wall-clock timeout
   - poll_quota: async poll iterations allowed
   - cost_quota: abstract "work units" allowed
   - priority: relative importance

2. **Budget combination**: min for limits, max for priority

3. **Backpressure = signal to reduce demand**
   When budget exhausted, callers must either:
   - Retry with fresh budget (if transient)
   - Propagate failure (if permanent)
   - Shed load (if graceful degradation enabled)

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Budget Types
```rust
pub struct Budget {
    pub deadline: Option<Instant>,
    pub poll_quota: Option<u64>,
    pub cost_quota: Option<u64>,
    pub priority: Priority,
}

impl Budget {
    pub fn combine(&self, other: &Budget) -> Budget {
        Budget {
            deadline: min_option(self.deadline, other.deadline),
            poll_quota: min_option(self.poll_quota, other.poll_quota),
            cost_quota: min_option(self.cost_quota, other.cost_quota),
            priority: max(self.priority, other.priority),
        }
    }

    pub fn is_exhausted(&self) -> bool {
        self.deadline.map_or(false, |d| Instant::now() > d)
            || self.poll_quota == Some(0)
            || self.cost_quota == Some(0)
    }
}
```

### 2. Integration Points
- **FUSE dispatch**: Per-request budget from config
- **ARC cache**: Eviction budget (prevent eviction storms)
- **MVCC commit**: Transaction budget
- **Scrub/GC**: Background budget (yields to foreground)
- **RaptorQ decode**: Symbol budget

### 3. Pressure Detection
```rust
pub struct PressureMonitor {
    pub fn sample_cpu_load() -> f64;
    pub fn sample_memory_pressure() -> f64;
    pub fn sample_io_depth() -> usize;
    pub fn compute_degradation_level() -> DegradationLevel;
}
```

### 4. Graceful Degradation FSM
```
Normal -> Pause Background -> Reduce Cache -> Throttle Writes -> Read-Only
   ^                                                              |
   +--------------------(pressure relieved)----------------------+
```

## TESTS
1. Unit: Budget combination semantics
2. Unit: Pressure detection thresholds
3. Integration: Simulated CPU load triggers degradation
4. Integration: Recovery when pressure relieved

## LOGGING
- Budget exhaustion (warn)
- Degradation level change (info)
- Pressure samples (trace)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Operations respect budget limits
2. Degradation level reflects actual pressure
3. Recovery is automatic and stable
4.  {"id":"bd-2s4","title":"EPIC: Production FUSE Runtime","description":"# EPIC: Production FUSE Runtime

## PURPOSE
Transform the FUSE interface from scaffold to production-ready mount runtime, as required by FEATURE_PARITY.md blocking gap #3.

## BACKGROUND
From spec §14 (FUSE Integration):
- Multi-threaded dispatch required
- All state must be Send + Sync
- Never block FUSE dispatch thread on raw I/O
- Proper lifecycle management (mount/unmount/signal handling)

Current state: "FUSE mount runtime: ❌ Interface scaffold only"

## CRITICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Mount Lifecycle
- Clean mount with explicit options (ro/rw, cache_size, etc.)
- Graceful unmount with flush and cleanup
- Signal handling (SIGTERM, SIGINT -> clean unmount)
- Crash recovery on restart

### 2. Multi-threaded Dispatch
- Use fuser multi-threaded mode
- Thread pool sizing based on CPU count
- Per-thread ARC cache access (Send + Sync)
- Connection pooling for concurrent requests

### 3. Error Handling
- Every FUSE op maps to correct errno
- Timeout handling for hung operations
- Partial failure recovery

### 4. Performance
- Request batching where applicable
- Readahead for sequential access
- Writeback coalescing
- Attribute caching with configurable TTL

### 5. Observability
- Per-op latency metrics
- Request queue depth
- Error rate tracking

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `ffs mount --rw /path/to/image.ext4 /mnt/ffs` works in production
2. Concurrent readers/writers do not deadlock
3. `umount /mnt/ffs` is clean (no data loss)
4. SIGTERM results in clean shutdown
5. All FUSE ops have correct errno mapping

## FILES
- crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs (main implementation)
- crates/ffs-fuse/src/mount.rs (lifecycle)
- crates/ffs-fuse/src/dispatch.rs (multi-thread)
- crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs (mount command)

## Success Criteria
1. All children closed
2. E2E suite (bd-3en1) passes
3. ffs mount --rw works on ext4 and btrfs images
4. SIGTERM unmount is clean (no data loss)
5. All FUSE ops have correct errno mapping","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:50:01.826764621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T09:22:39.072572592Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T09:22:39.072549739Z","close_reason":"Completed: all child beads closed and acceptance criteria validated by latest E2E artifacts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","fuse","reliability"],"comments":[{"id":91,"issue_id":"bd-2s4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW (lint fix): Epic is missing ## Success Criteria section.  {"id":"bd-2s4.1","title":"Implement FUSE write operations dispatch (create/mkdir/write/unlink/rename)","description":"# Implement FUSE write operations dispatch

## GOAL
Add write operation handlers to FrankenFuse so that mounted filesystems support mutation operations.

## BACKGROUND
Currently ffs-fuse only handles read operations (getattr, lookup, readdir, read, readlink). Write operations need:
1. FUSE protocol handlers in FrankenFuse
2. Delegation to FsOps trait methods
3. Transaction wrapping (begin → operation → commit)
4. Error mapping for write-specific errors (ENOSPC, EROFS, EEXIST, etc.)

## DELIVERABLES
### Phase 1: File creation and writing
1. `create(parent, name, mode, umask, flags)` → create file + open
2. `write(ino, fh, offset, data, flags)` → write data to file
3. `setattr(ino, mode, uid, gid, size, atime, mtime)` → chmod/chown/truncate

### Phase 2: Directory operations  
4. `mkdir(parent, name, mode, umask)` → create directory
5. `unlink(parent, name)` → remove file
6. `rmdir(parent, name)` → remove directory
7. `rename(parent, name, newparent, newname, flags)` → move/rename

### Phase 3: Link operations
8. `link(ino, newparent, newname)` → hard link
9. `symlink(parent, name, link)` → symbolic link
10. `mknod(parent, name, mode, rdev)` → device/fifo/socket

### Each handler must:
- Check read-only mode → EROFS
- Begin MVCC transaction
- Delegate to FsOps implementation
- Commit or abort transaction
- Map errors to errno
- Log operation at appropriate level

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Unit: create + write + read roundtrip
2. Unit: mkdir + create file inside + readdir
3. Unit: unlink reduces link count
4. Unit: rmdir on non-empty → ENOTEMPTY
5. Unit: rename across directories
6. Unit: EROFS on read-only mount
7. Unit: ENOSPC when disk full (simulated)
8. Integration: 100 concurrent file creates
9. Property: Random create/delete sequences maintain consistency

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
### Operation-level logging (all at debug unless error)
- create (debug): parent_ino, name, mode, result_ino
- write (trace): ino, offset, len, written_bytes (not data content)
- setattr (debug): ino, changed_fields (mode/size/time), old_values, new_values
- mkdir (debug): parent_ino, name, mode, result_ino
- unlink (debug): parent_ino, name, target_ino, remaining_links
- rmdir (debug): parent_ino, name, target_ino
- rename (debug): old_parent, old_name, new_parent, new_name, flags

### Transaction logging
- txn_begin (trace): txn_id, operation, inode(s)
- txn_commit (trace): txn_id, commit_seq, duration_us
- txn_abort (debug): txn_id, reason

### Error logging
- write_error (warn): operation, ino, errno, error_context
- permission_denied (info): operation, ino, uid, required_mode
- disk_full (warn): operation, requested_blocks, available_blocks

### Performance logging
- slow_operation (info): operation, ino, duration_ms (if > 100ms)

## POSIX COMPLIANCE NOTES
- create: EEXIST if exists and O_EXCL, ENOSPC if disk full
- mkdir: EEXIST, EACCES for permission
- unlink: EISDIR (use rmdir instead), ENOENT
- rmdir: ENOTEMPTY, ENOTDIR
- rename: atomic replace semantics (POSIX.1-2017)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All 10 FUSE write operations implemented
2. Transaction wrapping correct (commit on success, abort on error)
3. Read-only mount correctly rejects writes with EROFS
4. Errno mapping matches POSIX expectations
5. All operations logged with sufficient context for debugging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:52:34.624724548Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T19:18:08.152211645Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T19:18:08.152137927Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2ad","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:01.857993659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:52:34.624724548Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-huh.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:01.951296010Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":44,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"POSIX COMPLIANCE: Each write operation must handle specific error cases: create → EEXIST if exists, ENOSPC if disk full; mkdir → EEXIST, EACCES; unlink → EISDIR (use rmdir instead), ENOENT; rmdir → ENOTEMPTY, ENOTDIR; rename → atomic replace semantics. Reference POSIX.1-2017 for exact behavior.","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:57:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2s4.2","title":"Implement FUSE setxattr and removexattr operations","description":"# Implement FUSE setxattr and removexattr operations

## GOAL
Enable writing and removing extended attributes through the FUSE mount interface.

## BACKGROUND
listxattr and getxattr are already implemented (read-only). This bead adds the write side:
- setxattr: Create or replace an extended attribute
- removexattr: Delete an extended attribute

Requires inline xattr space management (may need to promote to external block if inline space exhausted).

## DELIVERABLES
1. FrankenFuse::setxattr() handler
2. FrankenFuse::removexattr() handler
3. Inline xattr space management (check if fits, promote if needed)
4. Integration with MVCC transaction wrapping

## TESTS
1. Set xattr on file, read it back
2. Replace existing xattr with larger value
3. Remove xattr, verify gone
4. Overflow inline space → external block (or ENOSPC if not supported)
5. EROFS on read-only mount

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. POSIX xattr semantics preserved
2. Transaction-wrapped for crash safety
3. No data corruption on inline/external boundary","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleElk","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:54:39.295902137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T20:11:54.424340962Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T20:11:54.424320825Z","close_reason":"Implemented FUSE setxattr/removexattr with core ext4 xattr mutation wiring, ENOSPC fallback, RO semantics, and tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:54:39.295902137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:54:39.398307753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":183,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).
